name: Remove QoS Reserved Bandwidth Limit
description: By default Windows reserves 20% of bandwidth for QoS/DSCP traffic. Removing this reservation allows all apps to use the full connection speed.

name: Disable IPv6
description: Disables IPv6 on all network adapters. Removes the IPv6 attack surface and can reduce DNS lookup delays on networks without IPv6 infrastructure. Reboot required.

name: Disable Network Location Pop-up
description: Suppresses the 'Do you want your PC to be discoverable?' pop-up that appears every time you connect to a new network.

name: Optimise DNS Cache TTL
description: Sets the DNS client cache max TTL to 86400 s (24 h) and negative cache to 300 s. Reduces repeat DNS lookups for frequently visited domains.

name: Disable Network Discovery (SSDP / UPnP)
description: Stops the SSDP Discovery, UPnP Host, and Function Discovery services that broadcast this machine's presence on the LAN. Reduces attack surface on public or untrusted networks.

name: Disable LMHosts Name Resolution
description: Disables the legacy LMHosts file used for NetBIOS name resolution. No modern application relies on this — disabling it reduces NetBIOS query broadcast noise.

name: Shorten TCP KeepAlive Interval
description: Reduces the TCP KeepAlive interval from 2 hours to 5 minutes. Detects dead connections faster, which improves resilience for apps that hold long-lived TCP sockets.

name: Disable TCP Timestamps
description: Disables the TCP timestamp option (RFC 1323). Timestamps can expose system uptime to network peers and marginally increase per-packet overhead. Low security improvement; mainly for hardening.

name: Enable DNS-over-HTTPS (DoH)
description: Configures DNS-over-HTTPS using Cloudflare (1.1.1.1) and Google (8.8.8.8) as secure resolvers. Encrypts DNS queries to prevent ISP and LAN surveillance.

steps.cmd: powershell -NoProfile -Command Set-DnsClientServerAddress -InterfaceIndex (Get-NetAdapter | Where Status -eq Up | Select -First 1 InterfaceIndex) -ServerAddresses 1.1.1.1,8.8.8.8

name: Disable SMB Compression
description: Prevents Windows from compressing data over SMB shares by default. Useful on fast networks where compression overhead outweighs bandwidth savings. Some legacy NAS devices also have issues with SMB compression.

name: Disable TCP/IP Offloading (Chimney)
description: Disables TCP Chimney offloading which can cause network instability on some NICs and routers. Recommended if you experience intermittent disconnections.

name: Set TCP Auto-Tuning to Normal
description: Sets the TCP receive window auto-tuning level to normal. Resolves slow network speeds on some routers that mishandle large windows.

steps.cmd: netsh interface tcp set global autotuninglevel=normal

name: Disable TCP Window Scaling
description: Disables TCP receive window scaling. Only use this as a diagnostic step for network problems — can reduce throughput on high-latency connections.

steps.cmd: netsh interface tcp set global rss=enabled autotuninglevel=normal

name: Enable TCP Quick ACK
description: Enables Quick ACK mode for faster acknowledgement of received data on per-connection basis. Improves responsiveness on high-latency connections.

name: Disable NetBIOS Name Service Broadcast
description: Stops the computer from broadcasting NetBIOS name queries to the entire local subnet. Reduces network noise and slightly improves sleep/wake times on networks with many devices.

name: Force SMBv2 Only (Disable SMBv1 Fallback)
description: Configures the SMB client to only negotiate SMBv2/v3. Adds extra protection against EternalBlue and other SMBv1 exploits without fully disabling SMB sharing.

name: Disable ICMP Redirect Acceptance
description: Prevents the system from accepting ICMP redirect packets. ICMP redirects can be abused to manipulate routing tables on LANs.

name: Disable Dead Gateway Detection
description: Prevents Windows from periodically testing alternate gateways listed in your routing table. Reduces background network activity on multi-NIC setups.

name: Enable DNS Prefetching
description: Enables DNS name prefetching so Windows proactively resolves domain names before you click links. Improves browsing responsiveness.

name: Disable NCSI Active Probing
description: Disables the active polling that Windows uses to check internet connectivity (downloading a file from msftconnect.com). Reduces background network activity but the status icon may show inaccurate results.

name: Disable Wake-on-LAN Magic Packets
description: Disables the NIC from waking the PC in response to Wake-on-LAN magic packets received over the network. Improves security on machines that must not be remotely awakened.

steps.cmd: powershell -NoProfile -Command Get-NetAdapter | Where Status -eq Up | ForEach-Object { Disable-NetAdapterWakeOnMagicPacket $_.Name -Confirm:$false -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }
